what_it_does: Renders a mobile-only bottom tray for the current pointed selection: it shows address chips and provides verb buttons to explain the selected items, hand them to the builder sheet as context, mint a change intent, or clear the selection.
observations: Uses pointedSet from AppContext and renders one chip per selected address, showing only the last path segment.
Implements four verbs: explain, ask, change, and clear.
Explain fetches /api/address-help for up to six addresses and displays title/represents/howto text, capped at 220 chars.
Ask builds a plain-text context block and dispatches a window CustomEvent named builder-context, then switches to the builder tab.
Change reveals an input + button that calls mintBuildIntent(changeText) and clears state on success.
Uses CSS classes mobile-tray, tray-chips, rhm-indicating, tray-chip, b, and tray-verbs.
The AppContext hook is cast to any.
Busy state is shared between explain and mint.
standouts: The large top comment frames the design: long-press points, then verbs act; 'Explain' is a verb, not a gesture.
Chips are treated as the context itself, avoiding extra narration.
One selection model is intended to cover chrome elements, canvas nodes, and forager circles.
CustomEvent builder-context is used to pass the selection block to the builder sheet.
conventions: React functional component with hooks (useState, useContext).
Async handlers set busy flags and update local state.
Fail-loud messaging when the registry fetch fails.
Class naming follows a kit/corpus vocabulary (rhm-indicating, b buttons).
concerns: AppContext is cast to any, hiding the real contract.
mintBuildIntent is called with only the text; the relationship to the selected set or last-pointed is not visible here.
No handling or feedback if mintBuildIntent fails or returns falsy.
Address chips display only the last segment, so different addresses with the same final segment look identical.
Busy is shared across explain and mint, which could cause odd UI if both are triggered in sequence.
Custom event name builder-context is a magic string with no local listener definition.
notes: A mobile selection-action tray that turns a long-press selection into chips and a small verb palette, bridging registry help and builder context.
questions: What is the exact shape and behavior of mintBuildIntent?
How is pointedSet populated and ordered, and what does last-pointed mean for mint?
Which component listens for the builder-context CustomEvent?
What does the rhm-indicating class signify and where is it defined?
Is there a non-mobile counterpart, and how does selection state sync between them?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the header matches the implementation: a slim mobile tray showing selected chips with explain/ask/change/clear verbs.
intention_signals: To make mobile pointing actionable by giving users a small, predictable post-selection menu where the selected items themselves supply context, avoiding complex gestures.
novelty: Treats 'Explain' as a deliberate verb after pointing rather than a long-press resolution, and uses the chip list itself as the narrative context for the builder.
contribution: Provides the mobile surface for acting on a shared pointed selection, including fetching registry descriptions, forwarding selection context to the builder, and initiating change intents.
summary_for_embedding: MobileTray.tsx is a React component that renders a mobile bottom tray of selected address chips with buttons to explain, ask, change, or clear the current selection.
intention_for_embedding: To give mobile users a compact post-long-press action tray where selected addresses become context for explanation, builder questions, and change intents.

symbols.does: Renders the mobile selection tray with chips and verb controls.
symbols.description: MobileTray is the exported React component that manages local verb state (explain/change), renders the current pointedSet as removable chips, and exposes buttons to explain, ask, change, or clear the selection.

symbols.does: Fetches registry help text for up to six selected addresses and displays the results.
symbols.description: The explain async function sets the verb to 'explain', iterates over the first six addresses in pointedSet, calls /api/address-help, and stores a short description for each address in the explained state.

symbols.does: Sends the selected addresses as a context block to the builder sheet and switches to the builder tab.
symbols.description: The ask function builds a plain-text block listing all pointed addresses, dispatches a window CustomEvent named builder-context with that block, and calls setMobileTab('builder') to open the builder sheet.

symbols.does: Submits the change input to mint a build intent and clears the input on success.
symbols.description: The mint async function trims the change input, calls mintBuildIntent with the text, and on a truthy response clears the input and collapses the change verb.
